data.title: Six Dead in Helicopter Crash in Hudson River Near New York City
data.description:
By Ginger Adams Otis and James Fanelli

Six people, including three children, died when a helicopter crashed into the Hudson River Thursday afternoon near New York City.

The aircraft went down in the vicinity of the West Side Highway and Spring Street on Manhattan's West Side, the New York Police Department said.

Six people were on board, according to New York Mayor Eric Adams: two adults and three children visiting from Spain, and the pilot.

What happened

The first calls came in around 3:17 p.m., according to the Fire Department. Multiple emergency response agencies were on the scene for rescue operations within minutes, authorities said. The aircraft landed in the water on the New Jersey side of the Hudson River, near Hoboken, officials said.

The helicopter was a Bell 206 helicopter. It departed from a downtown Manhattan heliport around 2:59 p.m. and traveled briefly south before turning north up the Hudson River.

It reached the George Washington Bridge at 3:08 p.m., said NYPD Commissioner Jessica Tisch. The helicopter then turned to travel southward along the New Jersey shoreline before it lost control and hit the water, Tisch said.

The helicopter appears to have been inverted when it crashed, she said.

The Federal Aviation Administration and the National Transportation Safety Board will investigate the cause of the crash. The NTSB will lead the investigation. President Trump said Transportation Secretary Sean Duffy would monitor investigation developments.

New York Helicopter

The aircraft was flying for New York Helicopter, a tour company, Tisch said.

New York Helicopter has faced financial challenges in recent months, court records show. Wynwood Capital Group, a cash-advance firm, sued New York Helicopter in February in state court, alleging that it advanced the tour company $50,000 in January in exchange for nearly $75,000 in future receivables. Days later, New York Helicopter blocked Wynwood from recouping the money, the lawsuit alleges.

PHI Aviation, a helicopter-services company, accused New York Helicopter in a federal lawsuit in January of defaulting on lease payments last year for a Bell 407 helicopter. PHI ultimately repossessed the leased helicopter in December, the lawsuit says.

New York Helicopter has yet to respond to either lawsuit in court filings. An exhibit in the PHI Aviation case includes an email from New York Helicopter's president, Michael Roth, complaining to PHI about the leased helicopter, saying that it broke down and wasn't operable for a week.

Records also show that Roth has operated New York Helicopter Charter, a company that filed for bankruptcy in 2019.

Previous crashes

NTSB records show that in 2015 an aircraft operated by New York Helicopter Charter crashed from about 20 feet off the ground. The hard landing occurred in Kearny, N.J. The aircraft was a Bell 206L-3, the NTSB report said, owned by Meridian Helicopters LLC but operated by New York Helicopter. A review of maintenance records revealed the helicopter had experienced a hard landing in Chile in 2010, as well.

In 2013, NTSB records show that a Bell 206 helicopter operated by New York Helicopter lost engine power during a sightseeing tour over the city. The pilot performed an emergency landing on the Hudson River. The pilot and four passengers stayed inside until help arrived and were unharmed, the report said.

Neither Roth nor the company New York Helicopter responded to a request for comment.

Write to Ginger Adams Otis at Ginger.AdamsOtis@wsj.com and James Fanelli at james.fanelli@wsj.com

data.title: Iron ore set for weekly loss as US-China trade war dents demand outlook
data.description:
Iron ore futures prices edged lower on Friday and were poised for a weekly loss, as escalating trade tensions between the United States and China - the world's two largest economies - clouded the outlook on demand.

The most-traded September iron ore contract on China's Dalian Commodity Exchange (DCE) traded 0.36% lower at 700.5 yuan ($95.73) a metric ton, as of 0156 GMT. It has lost 5.8% so far this week.

The benchmark May iron ore (SZZFK5) on the Singapore Exchange fell 1.07% to $96.1 a ton, bringing its weekly decline to 5.6%.

U.S. President Donald Trump hiked tariffs on Chinese imports to 125% soon after Beijing retaliated with lifting tariffs on American goods to 84% from 34% earlier.

Fears lingered whether China will respond in kind with even high tariffs.

Trade tensions showed no signs of easing, ANZ analysts said in a note, warning that a worst-case scenario could tip the global economy into a recession.

That has broadly weighed on sentiment in the metals market despite a short relief after Trump, in a stunning U-turn, announced a 90-day pause on the hefty duties for trading partners that didn't retaliate.

However, resilient near-term demand for iron ore and optimism over potential stimulus measures helped limit loss.

Average daily hot metal output, typically used to gauge iron ore demand, rose for a seventh consecutive week, climbing 0.6% from the previous week to a 17-month high of 2.4 million tons as of April 10, a survey from consultancy Mysteel showed.

Other steelmaking ingredients on the DCE lost further ground, with coking coal NYMEX:ACT1! and coke (DCJcv1) down 2.67% and 1.62%, respectively.

Steel benchmarks on the Shanghai Futures Exchange were mixed. Rebar was little changed, hot-rolled coil dipped 0.37%, stainless steel shed 0.43% while wire rod (SWRcv1) added 0.24%.

($1 = 7.3172 Chinese yuan)

data.title: Hong Kong Stocks Open Lower on US Tariffs, Slower GDP Growth Woes
data.description:
Hong Kong stocks opened lower on Friday after the White House clarified tariffs against China are at 145%.

The Hang Seng Index fell 1.14% to 20,446.35 on Friday morning from Thursday afternoon's close of 20,681.78. The Hang Seng China Enterprises Index declined 0.39% to 7,638.24 from 7,668.38.

Trump's latest executive order increased tariffs against China to 125% from 84%.

An official at the White House said the 125% tariff on Chinese imports comes on top of a 20% tariff imposed by the administration of US President Donald Trump related to fentanyl, according to a CNBC report.

Meanwhile, Trump's tariffs against China have made it "impossible for anyone to do business," the South China Morning Post reported Thursday, citing Steve Chuang Tzu-hsiung, chairman of the Federation of Hong Kong Industries.

"The biggest challenge for Hong Kong businesses now is, if you continue production in mainland China, do you ship or not? Will the American buyers cancel," the SCMP quoted Chuang as saying.

data.title: Simonds Group Appoints Rhett Simonds as CEO, Shares Fall 3%
data.description:
Simonds Group said its Chief Executive Officer David McKeown has stepped down, with immediate effect, according to a Friday Australian bourse filing.

Rhett Simonds was appointed CEO while retaining his role as Executive Chair, the filing said.

Simonds joined the Company in 2005 and was its CEO between February 2020 and November 2023.

The company's shares fell 3% in recent Friday trade.

data.title: Tesla has launched new ‘Long Range’ Cybertruck in the US @ $69990USD
data.description:
Tesla has unveiled a new version of its previously announced RWD Cybertruck.

  • will have a longer range
  • will lesser suspension and fewer features

This is the cheaper version of the truck. Up until now, Dual Motor AWD and Tri-motor AWD Cyberbeast have been available, This launch is of the Single Motor RWD.

data.title: Haven Assets Strengthen Amid Concerns About Trump Administration's Tariff PoliciesMarket Talk
data.description: Haven assets including gold and the Swiss franc strengthen amid continuing concerns about the Trump administration's tariff policies. "The trouble is, this Trump administration has conflicting objectives and challenging horizon mismatch (between geoeconomic ambitions and foreseeable economic/market outcomes)," Mizuho Securities (Singapore)'s Vishnu Varathan says in an email. "The complexity of myriad moving parts and the idiosyncrasies of this administration warn of a minefield of volatility flares," says the head of macro research for Asia ex-Japan. As such, the "primal instinct to look for cover, underpinning gold, CHF and JPY is understandable," Varathan adds. Spot gold rises 1.4% to $3,218.55/oz; USD/CHF falls 0.5% to 0.8199; USD/JPY is 0.6% lower at 143.54. (ronnie.harui@wsj.com)

data.title: Aussie technology stocks tumble on Wall St losses, but set for weekly gain
data.description:
** Australian technology stocks fall as much as 3.4% to 2187.3 points, but still on track for a 7.9% weekly gain to its best week since August 2024

** Sub-index's fall on the day in tandem with Wall Street decline as investors remain concerned about the economic implications of the ongoing trade war

** However, sub-index saw its biggest intraday percent gain since early November 2011 on Thursday amid a sharp rebound of global equities, allowing it to see an overall weekly gain

** Software co Nuix is the biggest percent loser on the sub-index after falling 12.1% to its lowest level since May 9, 2024

** Siteminder is down 4.6%, Wisetech falls as much as 3.9%, but still set for a 13.9% weekly gain

**YTD, sub-index down 19.1%, while the local benchmark S&P/ASX 200 is down 6.9%

data.title: New Zealand Dollar Extends Gains
data.description:
The New Zealand dollar climbed to around $0.578 on Friday, marking its third straight session of gains, driven by broad U.S. dollar weakness amid persistent economic concerns due to escalating tariff tensions.

While President Trump gave most countries a 90-day reprieve in enacting his reciprocal tariffs, he sharply increased tariffs on Chinese imports to 145%, heightening fears that Beijing could push retaliatory tariffs beyond the current 84%.

This also raised concerns for New Zealand, given its strong trade ties with China.

Domestically, the RBNZ recently cut its official cash rate by 25bps to 3.5%, as expected, citing a steady decline in inflation and weakening domestic economic conditions.

In economic news, the BusinessNZ PMI revealed that the country's manufacturing activity fell in March (53.2 vs 54.1 in February), although the sector remained in expansion for the third month in a row.

For the week, the Kiwi is up more than 3%, on track for its first weekly gain in four.

data.title: U.S.-China Economic Imbalances Need Adjustment to Resolve Trade KerfuffleMarket Talk
data.description: Persistent economic imbalances in the U.S. and China need to be addressed to resolve the current global trade kerfuffle, say economists at HSBC Global Research. The U.S. absorbs most of the world's surplus output of goods, while in China, spending by consumers remains too low and investment too high, say Frederic Neumann and Justin Feng in a note. To fix the situation, U.S. goods absorption will have to decline and saving rise, while China will need to curb its investment and boost domestic consumption, they say. While Europe and other economies have a role to play, the biggest adjustment will need come from the two protagonists--and that won't be resolved by tariffs and counter-tariffs, they add. (monica.gupta@wsj.com)

data.title: Ramelius Resources, Northern Star, Evolution Mining Shares Jump as Investors Flock to Gold Amid Global Trade Uncertainties
data.description:
Australian miners Ramelius Resources , Northern Star Resources , and Evolution Mining jumped around noon on Friday after US-China trade tensions prompted investors to seek safe-haven assets like gold.

Ramelius Resources advanced 0.6%; Northern Star jumped 3.8%; and Evolution Mining climbed 4.9% in recent trade.

Gold spot futures rose further by 1.2% to $3,215 per ounce on Friday morning after hitting an 18-month high on the prior day.

Markets remain clouded with uncertainties even as the US lowered the tariffs on most countries to 10% while excluding China.

data.title: Air New Zealand Repurchases Over 400,000 Ordinary Shares for Cancellation
data.description:
Air New Zealand repurchased for cancellation 425,990 ordinary shares, according to a filing with the New Zealand and Australian bourses.

The company acquired 110,453 ordinary shares on New Zealand's Stock Exchange at an average price of NZ$0.5935 each and 98,249 ordinary shares on the Australian Securities Exchange for an average price of AU$0.5398 apiece.

Additionally, 217,288 ordinary shares were acquired off-market from the finance minister at an average price of NZ$0.5906.

data.title: China, Japan, S.Korea central bank deputies discussed US tariffs
data.description:
China, Japan and South Korea finance and central bank officials met and discussed the impact of U.S. tariffs on the global and regional macroeconomic situation, China's central bank said on Friday.

The People's Bank of China Deputy Governor Xuan Changneng was at the ASEAN and China, Japan and South Korea finance and central bank deputies meeting on April 8-9, according to a statement.

data.title: WiseTech Bull Moderates Outlook on Product DelayMarket Talk
data.description: The likelihood that WiseTech Global won't release its Container Transport Optimization product until late in the June half prompts its bull at Bell Potter to moderate his revenue forecasts. Analyst Chris Savage keeps a buy rating on the stock, but warns that there is even a chance that the logistics-software provider's new product will not be rolled out until the December half. He therefore takes a conservative view of the product's likely revenue contribution, which drives EPS forecast downgrades of 1% for fiscal 2025, 3% for fiscal 2026, and 4% for fiscal 2027. Bell Potter trims its target price 8.2% to A$112.50. Shares are down 2.1% at A$83.47. (stuart.condie@wsj.com)

data.title: Iron Ore Prices Fall Amid Uncertainties Over U.S. TariffsMarket Talk
data.description: Iron ore prices are lower in early Asia trade, weighed by uncertainties surrounding U.S. tariffs. Global supply levels have fallen slightly while demand remains solid, Everbright Futures analysts write in a note. The current inventories have also fallen, while short-term prices are significantly affected by macroeconomic developments, they add. The analysts expect more near-term volatility. The most-traded iron ore contract on the Dalian Commodity Exchange is down 1.1% at CNY695.50 a ton. (jiahui.huang@wsj.com; @ivy_jiahuihuang)

data.title: China's Xi to Visit Asean as Beijing Looks to Bolster Trade TiesMarket Talk
data.description: China is doubling down on efforts to firm trade ties with Asian neighbors with top-level foreign visits amid a deepening tariff war with the U.S. Chinese leader Xi Jinping will tour Southeast Asia next week, stopping by Vietnam, Malaysia and Cambodia, the Chinese foreign ministry has said. The high-stake visits come after a top-level meeting this week when the Chinese leadership pledged to deepen economic integration and strengthen supply-chain cooperation with the rest of the Asian region. Senior Chinese officials have also reached out to the European Union, Australia and Saudi Arabia, offering to "join hands" to cope with Trump tariffs. Analysts say Trump's tariff salvos may risk pushing American allies further into China's orbit. The upcoming visits will be Xi's first abroad this year. (singaporeeditors@dowjones.com)

data.title: Japan futures set for largest weekly drop since 2020 on US-Sino trade war
data.description:
• Japanese rubber futures pulled back on Friday, and were set for their largest weekly losses since late 2020, amid a widening trade war between the U.S. and top consumer China.

  • The Osaka Exchange (OSE) September rubber contract , fell 2.42% to 290.9 yen ($2.03) per kg as of 0201 GMT.

  • The contract has lost 9.29% so far this week, its largest weekly drop since December 7, 2020.

  • The May rubber contract on the Shanghai Futures Exchange (SHFE) dipped 0.17% to 14,705 yuan ($2,009.68) per metric ton.

  • The most active May butadiene rubber contract on the SHFE (SHBRv1) gained 0.7% to 11,555 yuan ($1,579.18) per metric ton.

  • U.S. President Donald Trump paused for 90 days most of the hefty duties he had imposed on dozens of countries, but boosted tariffs on Chinese imports to 145% from the 104% level that kicked in on Wednesday.

  • Earlier, Beijing announced an additional import levy on U.S. goods on Wednesday, imposing an 84% tariff.

  • The direct impact of tariffs on natural rubber is mainly in Chinese tire exports to the U.S., Chinese financial information site Tonghuashun Information said, adding that the expected demand for rubber is under pressure amid fears of a global recession.

  • China's car exports will likely face greater-than-forecast pressure this year as U.S. tariff hikes hit economies of its important overseas markets hard, a Chinese auto industry association said.

  • The dollar slid 0.9% to 143.10 yen , the weakest since October 1.

  • A stronger currency makes yen-denominated assets less affordable to overseas buyers.

  • The front-month rubber contract on the Singapore Exchange's SICOM platform for May delivery last traded at 164 U.S. cents per kg, down 1%.

($1 = 143.5100 yen)

($1 = 7.3171 Chinese yuan)

data.title: Tesla launches new Cybertruck variant in US, priced at $69,990
data.description:
Tesla on Thursday said it has launched a new version of Cybertruck in the United States, priced at $69,990.

The new Cybertruck long-range variant is the cheapest among the three models it currently has in the U.S., Tesla's website shows.

data.title: Hero MotoCorp Forms Wholly-Owned Subsidiary in Brazil
data.description:
Indian two-wheeler manufacturer Hero MotoCorp has incorporated a wholly-owned subsidiary in Brazil named Hero MotoCorp Do Brasil, according to a Thursday filing to the Indian stock exchanges.

The Brazilian subsidiary will deal in the manufacturing and distribution of two-wheeler vehicles as well as parts and accessories.

data.title: Jade Gas advances Red Lake development in Mongolia with second successful well
data.description: Jade Gas remains on target to fast-track development of its Red Lake gas field in the South Gobi region of Mongolia after further drilling success. Jade has identified a high-value gas opportunity in Mongolia and is on track to quickly supply local buyers from what it considers a world-class asset. The Red Lake field sits in the Tavan Tolgoi Basin, a region with high demand for electricity, fuel and the base load energy supply necessary to underpin the region’s intermittent renewables. Future gas producer Announcing the successful drilling of a second production well at Red Lake, executive chair Dennis Morton said the results confirmed Jade’s confidence in the field being a future world-class gas producer. “This is a wonderful situation for the company as all gas sales options target high energy replacement costs,” Mr Morton said. “This second well outshines the first well with higher gas readings supporting the company’s desire to quickly commercialise the gas [and] we will now look to put the two into production within the next few weeks and move on to drilling the next wells.” Commercial pathway Jade now expects to complete an agreement with a midstream manufacturer of specialist compressed natural gas (CNG) equipment, which will enable the company to commercialise the gas from these initial wells. “The CNG facilities are easily transportable and are able to take lightly processed gas and convert it into a saleable and marketable form, enabling the company to potentially deliver to various markets in the immediate vicinity,” Mr Morton said. “Our gas commercialisation plan is built around a modular and scalable LNG project but we are also canvassing other nearer-term and lower-capex opportunities in the form of CNG, a product that is also utilised in displacing diesel in coal haulage trucks and which we expect will be in great demand,” Mr Morton said. “These markets are in need of a cleaner and more secure fuel source, including the displacement of diesel which currently powers the large coal haulage truck fleet in the South Gobi.”

data.title: Bitcoin Holds The Line—But Can It Bounce Back or Break Lower?
data.description:
Bitcoin price started a fresh increase above the $80,000 zone. BTC is now correcting gains and might struggle to stay above the $79,500 support.

  • Bitcoin started a fresh increase above the $80,000 zone.
  • The price is trading above $79,500 and the 100 hourly Simple moving average.
  • There is a new connecting bearish trend line forming with resistance at $80,500 on the hourly chart of the BTC/USD pair (data feed from Kraken).
  • The pair could start another increase if it clears the $80,500 zone.

Bitcoin Price Dips Again

Bitcoin price started a fresh increase above the $77,500 zone. BTC formed a base and gained pace for a move above the $79,500 and $80,000 resistance levels.

The bulls pumped the price above the $82,500 resistance. A high was formed at $83,548 and the price recently started a downside correction. There was a move below the $81,500 support. The price dipped below the 23.6% Fib retracement level of the upward move from the $74,572 swing low to the $83,548 high.

Bitcoin price is now trading above $79,200 and the 100 hourly Simple moving average. On the upside, immediate resistance is near the $80,500 level. There is also a new connecting bearish trend line forming with resistance at $80,500 on the hourly chart of the BTC/USD pair.

The first key resistance is near the $81,500 level. The next key resistance could be $82,500. A close above the $82,500 resistance might send the price further higher. In the stated case, the price could rise and test the $83,500 resistance level. Any more gains might send the price toward the $85,000 level.

More Losses In BTC?

If Bitcoin fails to rise above the $80,500 resistance zone, it could continue to move down. Immediate support on the downside is near the $79,500 level. The first major support is near the $79,000 level and the 50% Fib retracement level of the upward move from the $74,572 swing low to the $83,548 high.

The next support is now near the $78,000 zone. Any more losses might send the price toward the $76,500 support in the near term. The main support sits at $75,000.

Technical indicators:

Hourly MACD – The MACD is now losing pace in the bullish zone.

Hourly RSI (Relative Strength Index) – The RSI for BTC/USD is now above the 50 level.

Major Support Levels – $79,500, followed by $79,000.

Major Resistance Levels – $80,500 and $81,500.

data.title: Malaysia Palm Oil Outlook Likely to Stay Positive Despite U.S. TariffsMarket Talk
data.description: U.S. tariffs are likely to help palm oil demand in Malaysia in the medium term, especially if the U.S. shifts imports of palm oil from Indonesia on lower tariffs, RHB analysts say in a note. Before announcing a 90-day pause on the so-called reciprocal tariffs on Wednesday, the U.S. briefly imposed a 32% tariff on imports from Indonesia and 24% on goods from Malaysia. Demand could also rise if China substitutes U.S. soybeans with palm oil, following Beijing's 84% retaliatory tariffs on U.S. products, they say. Palm oil demand is expected to pick up in April as pricing becomes more attractive compared to soybean oil, they add. RHB maintains an overweight rating on the region's plantation sector, naming Johor Plantations, Sarawak Oil Palms, Bumitama Agri, London Sumatra Indonesia and SD Guthrie as its top picks. (yingxian.wong@wsj.com)

data.title: Mitsubishi Corporation makes DennoKotsu an equity-method affiliate company, strengthening efforts to address regional transportation challenges
data.description:
TOKYO, Apr 11, 2025 - (JCN Newswire) - Mitsubishi Corporation (MC) is pleased to announce its participation in a Series D round of funding, involving a third-party allotment of shares underwritten by 12 companies, conducted by DennoKotsu Inc. As a result of this acquisition, DennoKotsu, which promotes the digital transformation of taxi dispatch operations, will become an equity-method affiliate of MC.

In cooperation with local governments and transportation companies, MC has been working to address regional transportation challenges in Japan through AI-equipped on-demand bus and autonomous driving projects. MC previously made investments in DennoKotsu in 2020 and 2023, which have helped promote the digital transformation of its taxi dispatch business and call center services. By leveraging DennoKotsu's achievements, expertise, and practical knowledge in the taxi industry, along with MC's extensive business network, cooperation with other MC-affiliated companies has already begun. DennoKotsu's dispatch system is currently in use in 47 prefectures across Japan, supporting approximately 600 companies and 21,000 vehicles.

MC believes that automating transportation is a key solution to Japan's growing driver shortage. In anticipation of autonomous vehicles becoming more prevalent, DennoKotsu is expected to expand its operations beyond the taxi sector to the broader transportation field. This expectation led to MC's additional funding for DennoKotsu. With this investment, MC aims to further strengthen its collaboration with DennoKotsu and accelerate cooperation with on-demand bus services and autonomous driving projects. Through this, the company will provide comprehensive solutions tailored to the needs of each region, advancing transportation problem-solving and the creation of new businesses.

Inquiry Recipient:

Mitsubishi Corporation

Telephone:+81-3-3210-2171

Source: Mitsubishi Corporation

Copyright 2025 JCN Newswire . All rights reserved.

data.title: PBOC, BoJ and BoK met to discuss the impact of Trump tariffs on the global economy
data.description:
This readout via the People's Bank of China:

  • China’s Xuan Changneng, Deputy Governor of the PBoC, attended the ASEAN+3 Finance and Central Bank Deputies Meeting in Kuala Lumpur.

  • He chaired discussions as China’s co-chair of the 10+3 Finance and Financial Cooperation Mechanism.

  • Key topics included:

    • Impact of US tariffs on global and regional macroeconomics.

    • Strengthening the Chiang Mai Initiative Multilateralization (CMIM).

    • Enhancing the role of the 10+3 Macroeconomic Research Office (AMRO).

    • Deepening regional policy coordination and improving the regional financial safety net.

  • The meeting adopted legal arrangements for RMB investment under the CMIM.

  • China, Japan, and South Korea also held a separate trilateral meeting to discuss economic and financial cooperation.

  • Xuan reaffirmed China’s commitment to a moderately loose monetary policy to support market stability and economic recovery.

  • He held bilateral talks with senior officials from South Korea and Singapore to discuss global uncertainty and its national impacts.

Bolding above is mine.

data.title: VHM Gets Green Light for Victoria Rare Earth and Mineral Sands Project; Shares Rise 7%
data.description:
VHM said Resources Victoria approved a mining license for its Goschen rare earth and mineral sands project, according to a Friday Australian bourse filing.

It will be entitled to mine the land covered by the license, explore for minerals, and construct mining facilities related to the mining operation.

The first production at Goschen is anticipated for late 2026. All necessary remaining secondary approvals to construct, operate, and close the project are progressing and the firm will also finalize investment talks and develop strategic partnerships.

It recently appointed an independent technical, environmental, and social due diligence review for the project financing.

Its shares rose 7% in recent trading on Friday.

data.title: South Korean Shares Down at Friday's Open Amid Escalated Trade War
data.description:
Southe Korean shares opened lower on Friday, mirroring regional declines as investor sentiment soured following the Nasdaq's slump and heightened U.S.-China trade friction.

The Korea Composite Stock Price Index or Kospi opened 44 points or 1.8% lower at 2,400.74 before retracing its decline to trade 40.11 points, or nearly 1.6% lower at 2,406.21 at 10:58 am KST. The Kosdaq shed 0.20 points, or about 0.03%, to 681.60 at 10:58 am KST.

As a tech-heavy market vulnerable to global risk appetite, the Kospi faced selling pressure amid growing concerns about monetary tightening and corporate earnings uncertainty.

Meanwhile, trade tensions between the two largest economies heightened as the White House clarified that tariffs on Chinese imports is now at least 145%, up from 125% disclosed Wednesday.
